flashcards.back: Upfront investment in physical infrastructure, hardware, and software. Organizations purchase and own resources, bearing high initial costs and maintenance responsibilities.
Pay-as-you-go model where users pay only for consumed resources. Costs are treated as operational expenses rather than capital investments.
A computing service model where resources (storage, processing, applications) are provided as metered services, similar to electricity or telephone utilities, available on-demand.
A delivery model for IT services over the internet on-demand, providing shared computing resources (servers, storage, databases, networking) with pay-per-use pricing.

flashcards.front: List the five essential characteristics of cloud computing
flashcards.back: 1) On-demand self-service, 2) Broad network access, 3) Resource pooling, 4) Rapid elasticity, 5) Measured service (pay-per-use).
Users can automatically provision computing resources (storage, processing) as needed without requiring human interaction with service providers.
Computing resources are pooled to serve multiple customers using a multi-tenant model, with physical and virtual resources dynamically assigned based on demand.
Ability to quickly scale resources up or down automatically to meet demand, giving the impression of unlimited resources available on-demand.
Multiple customers (tenants) share shared physical infrastructure while their data and workloads remain isolated and secure from other tenants.

flashcards.front: Name the three main Cloud Service Models
flashcards.back: 1) Infrastructure as a Service (IaaS), 2) Platform as a Service (PaaS), 3) Software as a Service (SaaS).
Virtualized computing resources over the internet. Examples: Amazon EC2, Microsoft Azure VMs, Google Compute Engine.
Platform for application development and deployment. Examples: Google App Engine, Microsoft Azure App Service, Heroku.
Software applications delivered over the internet on subscription. Examples: Google Workspace, Microsoft 365, Salesforce, Dropbox.

flashcards.front: What are the four Cloud Deployment Models?
flashcards.back: 1) Public Cloud, 2) Private Cloud, 3) Hybrid Cloud, 4) Community Cloud.
Cloud services provided by third-party providers over the internet, available to anyone. Resources are shared across multiple organizations (multi-tenant).
